creator: madpilot@FreeBSD.org
text:
(In reply to John W. O'Brien from comment #0)
> Expected behavior
> -----------------
> 
> Installing the net/ntopng port will pull in all dependencies required for
> the typical configuration.

This is not really a correct expectation. It pulls in all the dependencies actually required by the port to start without crashing. Further configuration requirement for the machine are not covered.

In this case even adding a redis dependency would not start the redis server for you, so, if skipping that startup step by ignoring pkg-message instructions, would put you in front of the same error message. (ports cannot start a daemon for you)

> 
> 
> Observed behavior
> -----------------
> 
> net/ntopng nominally requires databases/redis but registers no dependency on
> it.
> 
> % pkg info -D ntopng  | grep -A2 requires
> ntopng requires to connect to a redis server to work. Please install redis
> server from databases/redis or use -r option via ntopng_flags to specify a
> remote one.

It does not require the redis port to be installed on the same machine. NTOPng just requires a redis database to connect to, which could also reside on another machine/jail.

(I for example have redis in a separated jail, and would not like to keep it installed in the ntop jail just to satisfy a package requirement)

[...]
> How to reproduce
> ----------------
> 
> 1. pkg install net/ntopng
> 2. Read the pkg-message

Which implies following instructions in there.

Requirement for a redis DB to connect to is in the second paragraph.

> 3. Check the dependencies of net/ntopng
> 
> 
> Work-around
> -----------
> 
> 1. pkg install databases/redis

As stated before this really is step 3 in the previous list.

This could apply to any port using potgresql. If the port to work requires connecting to an SQL DB you will need to actually install and configure the database engine before running it.

The postgres and mysql ports are separated in a client and server parts because almost all ports using them link to their client library. ntopng does not link to an external library and talks directly to the redis database, but installing a server is always an external requirement, which some ports don't even state in their pkg-message

> 
> 
> Fix
> ---
> 
> 
> Option 1:
> 
>     Unconditionally define RUN_DEPENDS=redis>=0:databases/redis
> 
> Option 2:
> 
>     Define a new OPTION called REDIS that is enabled by default and define
> REDIS_RUN_DEPENDS=redis>=0:databases/redis

Option 1 is plainly wrong, since the DB can be installed also elsewhere. I did think about adding this option when I took the port and intentionally decided it was wrong for all these reasons.

Option 2 would just be confusing:

for people using the ports the option page comes before the pkg-message is shown them, so they would not have information on what to do with that option(talking about people who have not already faced this situation)

for people using official packages, we would be forcing them in the behaviour we choose when we decide if the option should default to on.

I consider the redis dependency like a database dependency. If compiling the software requires a client library I depend on that, but I do not add a "server" dependency, it's an administrator's work to choose where and how to setup a DB server in their system/network. It's a completely different decision with which package management has nothing to do.


Unless compelling logic voids all the above points I'd consider this solved as "works as intended", but I don't like to shut PRs in the face of submitters, so I'd like to hear back from you before marking it closed.
